creator: freebsd-ports@charlieroot.de
text:
Starting snmpd with '-C' helps though I definitely did not specify agentaddress twice:

[helmut@BSDHelmut ~]$ for FILE in $(sudo kdump -f ./ktrace.out | grep -Eo '"/.*\.conf"' | tr -d '"' | sort -u); do sudo grep -Ei '(agentaddress|trap)' $FILE; done
grep: /etc/malloc.conf: No such file or directory
agentaddress udp6:161
agentaddress udp:161
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.0.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.1.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.10.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.2.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.3.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.4.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.5.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.6.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.7.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.8.conf: No such file or directory
grep: /var/net-snmp/snmpd.9.conf: No such file or directory
[helmut@BSDHelmut ~]$
